# Work Packages: Repositories + Indexes

**Inputs**: Design documents from `kitty-specs/002-repositories-and-indexes/`
**Prerequisites**: plan.md (required), spec.md (user stories), research.md, data-model.md, contracts/
**Tests**: No automated tests (manual testing for v1 per constitution).

**Organization**: Fine-grained subtasks (`Txxx`) roll up into work packages (`WPxx`). Each work package must be independently deliverable and testable.

**Prompt Files**: Each work package references a matching prompt file in `kitty-specs/002-repositories-and-indexes/tasks/` generated by `/spec-kitty.tasks`.

## Subtask Format: `[Txxx] [P?] Description`
- **[P]** indicates the subtask can proceed in parallel (different files/components).
- All source file paths are relative to `Reppo/` (the Xcode project root).

## Path Conventions
- **Protocols**: `Reppo/Core/Repositories/Protocols/`
- **Implementations**: `Reppo/Core/Repositories/`
- **Models (existing)**: `Reppo/Data/Models/`
- **Enums (existing)**: `Reppo/Data/Enums/`
- **App entry point**: `Reppo/App/ReppoApp.swift`

---

## Work Package WP01: Repository Protocols + SetSortOrder Enum (Priority: P0)

**Goal**: Create all 8 repository protocol files and the `SetSortOrder` supporting enum. These define the complete data-access contract for the repository layer. No SwiftData import — protocols use Foundation types and reference existing @Model types.
**Independent Test**: All 8 protocol files compile. No SwiftData import in any protocol file. Every method signature matches the contracts in `kitty-specs/002-repositories-and-indexes/contracts/`.
**Prompt**: `kitty-specs/002-repositories-and-indexes/tasks/WP01-repository-protocols.md`
**Estimated prompt size**: ~400 lines

### Included Subtasks
- [x] T001 [P] Create `SetSortOrder` enum in `Reppo/Core/Repositories/Protocols/SetSortOrder.swift`
- [x] T002 [P] Create `SetRepositoryProtocol` in `Reppo/Core/Repositories/Protocols/SetRepositoryProtocol.swift`
- [x] T003 [P] Create `WorkoutRepositoryProtocol` in `Reppo/Core/Repositories/Protocols/WorkoutRepositoryProtocol.swift`
- [x] T004 [P] Create `ExerciseRepositoryProtocol` in `Reppo/Core/Repositories/Protocols/ExerciseRepositoryProtocol.swift`
- [x] T005 [P] Create `ExerciseStatsRepositoryProtocol` in `Reppo/Core/Repositories/Protocols/ExerciseStatsRepositoryProtocol.swift`
- [x] T006 [P] Create `PerformanceRecordRepositoryProtocol` in `Reppo/Core/Repositories/Protocols/PerformanceRecordRepositoryProtocol.swift`
- [x] T007 [P] Create `BodyweightEntryRepositoryProtocol` in `Reppo/Core/Repositories/Protocols/BodyweightEntryRepositoryProtocol.swift`
- [x] T008 [P] Create `HealthProfileRepositoryProtocol` in `Reppo/Core/Repositories/Protocols/HealthProfileRepositoryProtocol.swift`
- [x] T009 [P] Create `ProgramRepositoryProtocol` in `Reppo/Core/Repositories/Protocols/ProgramRepositoryProtocol.swift`

### Implementation Notes
- All protocols conform to `Sendable` (enables crossing actor boundaries)
- All methods are `async throws`
- Protocols reference @Model types (WorkoutSet, Workout, etc.) but do NOT import SwiftData
- `SetSortOrder` is a `Sendable` enum used by `SetRepositoryProtocol.fetchSets(for:reps:orderedBy:)`
- Contract files in `kitty-specs/002-repositories-and-indexes/contracts/` are the source of truth for method signatures

### Parallel Opportunities
- All 9 files are independent — all subtasks can proceed in parallel

### Dependencies
- None (starting package). Requires feature 001 @Model classes to exist for type references.

### Risks & Mitigations
- Protocol files may need `import SwiftData` if Swift can't resolve @Model types without it → Test compilation; add import only if compiler requires it, with a comment explaining why.

---

## Work Package WP02: Core Repository Implementations (Priority: P1) 🎯 MVP

**Goal**: Implement the 3 most complex repositories (SetRepository, PerformanceRecordRepository, BodyweightEntryRepository) using `@ModelActor`. These contain the specialized query methods required by the PR pipeline and effectiveWeight calculation — the critical path for features 003-005.
**Independent Test**: All 3 repository actors compile and conform to their protocols. `@ModelActor` macro generates correct serial executor. fetchMaxEffectiveWeight uses sort+fetchLimit(1) pattern. Closest-weight lookup uses two-query algorithm.
**Prompt**: `kitty-specs/002-repositories-and-indexes/tasks/WP02-core-repository-implementations.md`
**Estimated prompt size**: ~500 lines

### Included Subtasks
- [x] T010 Create `SetRepository` actor in `Reppo/Core/Repositories/SetRepository.swift` with all CRUD + aggregation methods
- [x] T011 Create `PerformanceRecordRepository` actor in `Reppo/Core/Repositories/PerformanceRecordRepository.swift` with PR lookup methods
- [x] T012 Create `BodyweightEntryRepository` actor in `Reppo/Core/Repositories/BodyweightEntryRepository.swift` with closest-weight lookup

### Implementation Notes
- All 3 use `@ModelActor` macro — imports SwiftData, uses `modelContext` directly
- `SetRepository.fetchMaxEffectiveWeight`: sort by effectiveWeight DESC + `fetchLimit = 1` (database-level MAX equivalent)
- No `fetchTotalVolume` on SetRepository — callers read `ExerciseStats.totalVolume` (pre-computed at write-time by StatsService).
- `PerformanceRecordRepository.fetch(exerciseId:recordType:reps:)`: predicate on 3 fields + fetchLimit 1 (O(1) PR lookup)
- `BodyweightEntryRepository.fetchClosest`: two queries (nearest before + nearest after), compare absolute time distance
- Inside actor, methods omit `async` keyword — Swift auto-bridges to async at call site
- No `#Index` on iOS 17 (documented limitation)

### Parallel Opportunities
- T010, T011, T012 are independent files and can proceed in parallel after WP01 protocols exist.

### Dependencies
- Depends on WP01 (protocols must exist for conformance).

### Risks & Mitigations
- `#Predicate` macro limitations with optional fields → Test predicates against nullable fields (reps: Int?, effectiveWeight: Double?) carefully. Use explicit nil checks in predicates.
- `@ModelActor` initialization requires `ModelContainer` → Implementations should have `init(modelContainer:)` auto-generated by macro. Verify compilation.

---

## Work Package WP03: Remaining Repositories + DI Wiring (Priority: P1)

**Goal**: Implement the 5 simpler CRUD repositories (WorkoutRepository, ExerciseRepository, ExerciseStatsRepository, HealthProfileRepository, ProgramRepository) and wire all 8 repositories into the app's dependency injection via ReppoApp.swift. Add iOS 18 index documentation as code comments.
**Independent Test**: All 8 repositories compile and conform to protocols. ReppoApp.swift instantiates all repositories from the shared ModelContainer. Project builds with zero errors. `ExerciseRepository.hasAssociatedSets` queries WorkoutSet table. `HealthProfileRepository.fetchOrCreate` creates default profile if none exists.
**Prompt**: `kitty-specs/002-repositories-and-indexes/tasks/WP03-remaining-repos-and-di-wiring.md`
**Estimated prompt size**: ~450 lines

### Included Subtasks
- [x] T013 [P] Create `WorkoutRepository` actor in `Reppo/Core/Repositories/WorkoutRepository.swift`
- [x] T014 [P] Create `ExerciseRepository` actor in `Reppo/Core/Repositories/ExerciseRepository.swift`
- [x] T015 [P] Create `ExerciseStatsRepository` actor in `Reppo/Core/Repositories/ExerciseStatsRepository.swift`
- [x] T016 [P] Create `HealthProfileRepository` actor in `Reppo/Core/Repositories/HealthProfileRepository.swift`
- [x] T017 [P] Create `ProgramRepository` actor in `Reppo/Core/Repositories/ProgramRepository.swift`
- [x] T018 Wire all 8 repositories in `Reppo/App/ReppoApp.swift` (DI wiring)
- [x] T019 Add iOS 18 `#Index` declarations as comments in `Reppo/Data/Models/PerformanceRecord.swift` and `Reppo/Data/Models/WorkoutSet.swift`

### Implementation Notes
- Repositories T013-T017 are straightforward CRUD with `@ModelActor`
- `WorkoutRepository.fetchInProgress()`: predicate on `status == .inProgress`, fetchLimit 1
- `ExerciseRepository.search(name:)`: use `localizedStandardContains` in `#Predicate` for case-insensitive search
- `ExerciseRepository.hasAssociatedSets(_:)`: query WorkoutSet (cross-entity query), fetchLimit 1, return non-empty
- `HealthProfileRepository.fetchOrCreate()`: fetch first; if nil, create HealthProfile with defaults and save
- **DI wiring (T018)**: create all 8 repository instances from shared `ModelContainer` in `ReppoApp.init()`, inject via SwiftUI `.environment`
- **Index comments (T019)**: add `// TODO: iOS 18 — #Index<PerformanceRecord>(...)` comment blocks showing exact syntax for future upgrade

### Parallel Opportunities
- T013-T017 are all independent files, fully parallelizable
- T018 (DI wiring) depends on all repositories existing
- T019 (index comments) is independent of other subtasks

### Dependencies
- Depends on WP01 (protocols) and WP02 (core implementations must exist for DI wiring to reference all 8 repositories).

### Risks & Mitigations
- `ExerciseRepository.hasAssociatedSets` queries a different entity (WorkoutSet) — verify `#Predicate` works across entities within the same `ModelContext`.
- `HealthProfileRepository.fetchOrCreate` performs insert+save if nil — ensure thread safety via `@ModelActor` serial execution.
- SwiftUI `.environment` injection for actors may require `@Observable` wrapper or custom `EnvironmentKey` — research during implementation, use manual init injection as fallback.

---

## Dependency & Execution Summary

- **Sequence**: WP01 → WP02 → WP03 (linear dependency chain)
- **Parallelization**: Within each WP, most subtasks are independent files ([P] marked). Across WPs, WP02 and WP03 both depend on WP01 but WP03 also depends on WP02 (DI wiring needs all repos).
- **MVP Scope**: WP01 + WP02 constitute the minimal release — protocols + the 3 critical repositories needed for features 003-005 (PR pipeline, stats, effectiveWeight).
- **Full Scope**: WP01 + WP02 + WP03 = complete repository layer with DI wiring.
